book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
Extracts from the Acts. Session VI.
title: Extracts from the Acts. Session VI.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: After this reading, the capitulas were handed by our most sacred and pious prince to the most beloved of God Anatolius, archbishop of royal Constantinople, which is New Rome, and all the most God-beloved bishops cried out: Many years to our Emperor and Empress, the pious, the Christian.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: May Christ whom thou servest keep thee.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: These things are worthy of the faith.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Thou hast straightened out the churches, victor of thine enemies, teacher of the faith.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Many years to the pious Empress, the lover of Christ.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Many years to her that is orthodox.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Ye have put down the heretics, ye have kept the faith.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: May hatred be far removed from your empire, and may your kingdom endure for ever!

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
verses.text: Our most sacred and pious prince said to the holy synod: To the honour of the holy martyr Euphemia, and of your holiness, we decree that the city of Chalcedon, in which the synod of the holy faith has been held, shall have the honours of a metropolis, in name only giving it this honour, the proper dignity of the city of Nicomedia being preserved.

verses.book: The Fourth Ecumenical Council. The Council of Chalcedon.
